title: Use adversarial validation and clipped importance weighting for train-test race-shift
summary: This train/test split spans multiple seasons and a shrinking driver pool, so covariate shift is plausible.
rationale: This train/test split spans multiple seasons and a shrinking driver pool, so covariate shift is plausible. Covariate-shift literature shows ordinary CV can misestimate generalization when train and test feature distributions differ; importance-weighted validation is a principled correction.
implementation_hint: Train a train-vs-test classifier on raw plus engineered features with Race-Year grouping preserved, inspect which races/years/drivers are most test-like, and derive clipped sample weights from the predicted density ratio. Use the weights only if they improve agreement between race-held-out CV and submission results.
expected_effect: May not raise local CV, but can improve leaderboard alignment and reduce over-selection of solutions that exploit obsolete historical regimes.
risk: Noisy weights can increase variance and overfit to the public test distribution; weight clipping and ablation are mandatory.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
